author: lOrm
content:
Fix #695: cap SSE per-line accumulator at MAX_SSE_LINE_BYTES = 1 MiB.

Forensic trace:
- src/proxy.rs: added pub const MAX_SSE_LINE_BYTES = 1024 * 1024 next to SSE_STREAM_TIMEOUT_SECS.
- src/pipeline.rs: added pure helper enforce_sse_line_cap(&mut String) -> SseLineCapOutcome (WithinCap | Exceeded { discarded_bytes }) plus a thin wrapper enforce_sse_line_cap_with_report that does the tracing::warn + AppEvent::ApiError emit.
- The stream_sse_response chunk-read branch now calls enforce_sse_line_cap_with_report immediately after buffer.push_str — so a hostile or broken upstream that never emits \n drops & resets the buffer when it crosses 1 MiB instead of growing it without bound until OOM. Valid frames containing \n are passed through untouched even when larger than the cap.
- 5 new tests pin the contract:
  1. issue_695_max_sse_line_bytes_constant_is_1mib (constant pin).
  2. issue_695_enforce_sse_line_cap_small_buffer_is_no_op (no false trip on partial frames).
  3. issue_695_enforce_sse_line_cap_bounds_unbounded_input (drives 2 MiB of newline-free chunks through the helper; asserts buffer.len() always stays below the cap, asserts the cap trips at least once, asserts cumulative discard >= cap).
  4. issue_695_enforce_sse_line_cap_skips_when_newline_present (cap does not fire on terminated frames larger than the cap).
  5. issue_695_enforce_sse_line_cap_reset_is_total (overflow buffer cleared in full, subsequent valid frame parses cleanly).

Verification:
- cargo test --lib: 1447 passed, 0 failed.
- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings: exit 0, zero warnings.
- No #[allow], no clippy.toml, no #[ignore].
